title: SFMC Tips #142 : Marketing Cloud Next : Automating Consent Creation
markdown:
[

![Nobuyuki Watanabe @marketingcloudtips](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fill:40:40/1*zLRnWqPVgEgwGDwBBiwV3w.png)



](/@marketingcloudtips?source=post_page---byline--78649b12da0e---------------------------------------)

10 min read

Jun 19, 2025

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*fMWIpsFFZA8gQd48)

Photo by [Sasha Kaunas](https://unsplash.com/@akaunas?utm_source=medium&utm_medium=referral) on [Unsplash](https://unsplash.com/?utm_source=medium&utm_medium=referral)

**Automating “Consent Creation”** in Marketing Cloud Next Growth & Advanced Editions based on record creation or updates is most simply and practically implemented by using the **“Prospect, Lead, Contact or Related Record Change”** event in an **Event-Triggered Flow**.

Therefore, in this article, I explain how to automatically create consent records when a Contact is created or when an email address is changed.

In addition, there are also implementation patterns that use **Data Cloud Triggered Flows**, so I will also introduce the differences and important considerations between these approaches.

## Discussion Around Disabling Consent Management

Marketing Cloud Next also includes a setting that allows you to disable the **consent management** feature.

Of course, it is possible to disable this feature and operate without performing any **“Consent Creation”** at all.

However, in that case, unsubscribe requests through **List Unsubscribe** can no longer be properly received.

In other words, even if a user unsubscribes directly from their email client, that action will effectively be **ignored**.

Therefore, even when not using the standard unsubscribe link or preference center, it still appears necessary to at least assign an **OPT-IN consent record** to all email addresses.

Personally, when it comes to promotional email delivery, I believe that completely avoiding the standard **Marketing Cloud Next consent management** functionality is fundamentally not a realistic option. 🤔

## Unsupported Consent Creation Methods

Salesforce has published official [help documentation](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=005316463&type=1) regarding this “Consent Creation” and warns about unsupported methods.

### Officially Supported Flows

- **Event-Triggered Flow**
- **Data Cloud Triggered Flow**

### Unsupported Flow Actions

- **MessagingConsent.MessagingConsent**
- **MessagingConsentV2.MessagingConsent**

If these unofficial actions are used, it appears possible for situations to occur where consent records visually exist, but **actual delivery control does not align correctly**.

For example:

- The consent record is **OPT-IN**, but sending is actually not possible
- The consent record is **OPT-OUT**, but sending is still possible

To avoid such inconsistencies, it is important to implement consent record creation using **methods officially supported by Salesforce whenever possible**.

## Event-Triggered Flow Configuration Steps

### Pre-Implementation Considerations

In actual organizations, customization based on each company’s consent management policies and data structure will be necessary.

In addition, the following cases are not considered in this article:

- Cases where multiple communication subscriptions exist
- Full synchronization between CRM-side opt-in status and existing consent records

This implementation is intentionally designed as a simple configuration focused solely on creating **OPT-IN consent records** for new email addresses.

1\. Open the **Event-Triggered Flow**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*_CsIdItBT4d7F1JX)

2\. In the event selection, choose “**Prospect, Lead, Contact or Related Record Change**”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*x--Q1tQUrKnZ2Vux)

3\. This time, select Contact and configure the settings as follows.

- **Object:** Contact
- **Trigger Condition:** “When a record is created or updated”

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*ynlBU_DgESOoz2Lf)

- **Entry Condition:**  
  Email（Field）  
  is Null（Operator）  
  False（Value）

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*D3rVms0xDIjl0oKx)

**_Important:_** _If you want to support Leads as well, you must configure the same flow separately for Lead._

4\. Next, place a **Get Records** element and retrieve matching records from the Communication Subscription Consent DMO using the email address (Contact Point Value) of the triggered record.

_※ This implementation does not assume cases where multiple communication subscriptions exist._

- **Element Name (Example)**  
  Get Consent Records
- **Data Source**  
  Data Cloud Object
- **Data Cloud Object**  
  Communication Subscription Consent
- **Search Conditions  
  **Communication Subscription Consent > Contact Point Value  
  Equals  
  {!$Input.Email}

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*WdJPWK3vkSBymEhv)

The triggered object becomes available as a variable in the **Flow Resource Panel** and can be referenced in subsequent actions. {!$Input.}

For the settings below:

- Sort Records
- How Many Records to Store
- How to Store Record Data

the default settings are sufficient.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*MFYuRUzPwY4IR03Q)

5\. Next, place a Decision element to branch between cases where the retrieved consent record email address and the triggered email address are “equal” and “not equal”.

This branching prevents re-opt-in when a consent record already exists as an opt-out.

- **Element Name (Example)**  
  Has Existing Consent Record
- **Branch Path Name (Example)**  
  Has Existing Consent Record
- **Condition  
  **{!Get\_Consent\_Records.ssot\_\_ContactPointValueText\_\_c}  
  Equals  
  {!$Input.Email}

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*KXkdq2i5Vnu5x88q)

6\. Finally, add the Create Consent element to the default path for people whose email addresses do not match, and configure the required fields.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*uOKbabUpteHmGRD9)

7\. After completing the Create Consent configuration using the following example as reference, save and activate the flow.

(Adjust these settings according to your company requirements.)

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*F9_3iwR0M2Q45Z_O)

**About Errors That Occur When Saving the Flow**

- If you receive the error **“Complete required fields.”**, and there are no issues with the configuration, try configuring the **Create Consent** element several times. In some cases, the **Communication Subscription** setting may become unset automatically. Sometimes exiting the flow once and reopening it resolves the issue.

## Data Cloud Triggered Flow Configuration Steps

The biggest advantage of a **Data Cloud Triggered Flow** is that you do not need to configure separate flows for Contacts and Leads.

Everything can be handled within a single flow.

However, there are confirmed cases where **Data Cloud Triggered Flows** behave unstably. For example:

- Differential updates work correctly, but the flow does not react during a full DSO refresh
- During large-scale updates, some records are skipped from processing

I have personally experienced these types of behaviors during actual testing and operations.

Therefore, in production environments, I strongly recommend designing and operating with monitoring and retry mechanisms rather than assuming that all records will always be processed successfully.

Also, as a reminder, please review the following pre-implementation considerations.

### Pre-Implementation Considerations

In actual organizations, customization based on each company’s consent management policies and data structure will be necessary.

In addition, the following cases are not considered in this article:

- Cases where multiple communication subscriptions exist
- Full synchronization between CRM-side opt-in status and existing consent records

This implementation is intentionally designed as a simple configuration focused solely on creating **OPT-IN consent records** for new email addresses.

1\. Open the **Data Cloud Triggered Flow**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*CZVzT48X8DDiGmus)

2\. Configure the Start element as follows.

- **Object:** Contact Point Email DMO
- **Trigger Condition:** “When a record is created or updated”
- **Entry Condition:** None

_※ According to the_ [_official help documentation_](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=platform.flow_ref_elements_mktg_consent_request.htm&type=5)_, the “_**_When a record is created_**_”_ **_option is currently not supported_**_._

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*L34jmWG2Qt-YyLX2)

**Important: Behavior of Data Cloud Triggered Flows**

## Get Nobuyuki Watanabe @marketingcloudtips’s stories in your inbox

Join Medium for free to get updates from this writer.

Remember me for faster sign in

The important point is that the trigger does not fire simply because records are added to or removed from this DMO. What is actually being monitored is whether the source profile data behind the data stream (such as Contact or Lead records) has been created or updated. In simple terms, the trigger reacts to differences compared to the previous data state.

Using Data Space Filters allows records to move in and out of the DMO, but this alone does not create a difference from the previous data state, so the trigger will not fire. If you want to reliably trigger the flow, create or update the target records on the CRM side.

3\. Next, place a Get Records element and retrieve matching records from the Communication Subscription Consent DMO using the email address (Contact Point Value) of the triggered record.

_※ This implementation does not assume cases where multiple communication subscriptions exist._

- **Element Name (Example)**  
  Get Consent Records
- **Data Source**  
  Data Cloud Object
- **Data Cloud Object**  
  Communication Subscription Consent
- **Search Conditions  
  **Communication Subscription Consent > Contact Point Value  
  Equals  
  {!$Record.ssot\_\_EmailAddress\_\_c}

For the settings below:

- Sort Records
- How Many Records to Store
- How to Store Record Data

the default settings are sufficient.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*u1hTSj_7jY2toP_6)

4\. Next, place a Decision element to branch between cases where the retrieved consent record email address and the triggered email address are “equal” and “not equal”.

This branching prevents re-opt-in when a consent record already exists as an opt-out.

- **Element Name (Example)**  
  Has Existing Consent Record
- **Branch Path Name (Example)**  
  Has Existing Consent Record
- **Condition  
  **{!Get\_Consent\_Records.ssot\_\_ContactPointValueText\_\_c}  
  Equals  
  {!$Record.ssot\_\_EmailAddress\_\_c}

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*H6Pt2yXSUeqfdyu1)

5\. Finally, add the Create Consent element to the default path for people whose email addresses do not match, and configure the required fields.

After selecting Action, search for **“Create Consent”.**

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*cwzUoaWcubR_tyHY)

6\. After completing the Create Consent configuration using the following example as reference, save and activate the flow.

(Adjust these settings according to your company requirements.)

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*mzH3HVBErp809PF8)

**About Errors That Occur When Saving the Flow**

- If you receive the error **“Complete required fields.”**, and there are no issues with the configuration, try configuring the **Create Consent** element several times. In some cases, the **Communication Subscription** setting may become unset automatically. Sometimes exiting the flow once and reopening it resolves the issue.
- If you receive the error: **“An unexpected error occurred. Please include this ErrorId if you contact support: 1073913089–99424 (700678262)”** the cause is that required DLO fields (such as **Id** in this example) are not mapped to the DMO being used in the trigger.

## Configuration Steps for Unsupported Actions

I will leave the following information here just in case, but in practice, these are not actually used.

- MessagingConsent.MessagingConsent Flow Action
- MessagingConsentV2.MessagingConsent Flow Action

If you use these, configure a **Scheduled Path** in the **Record-Triggered Flow** and execute the processing from that path.

Starting the scheduled path **1 minute after the Last Modified Date** should be sufficient.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*RfgDABOcizSwwITI)

Below, I will briefly explain how to configure these actions.

## How to Configure the “MessagingConsent” Action

In a **Record-Triggered Flow** or similar flow, place an **Action** element, search for **“Consent”**, and select **MessagingConsentV2.MessagingConsent**.

_If multiple versions are available, select the latest version._

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*bWPFdPt0crOOj1cO)

Next, retrieve the **Communication Subscription Channel Type ID**.

The **Communication Subscription Channel Type** is unique for each combination of a channel (such as Email) and a Communication Subscription.

You can temporarily display the following tab and search for it there.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*U6AYND-ckpSz5-5g)

Open the target **Communication Subscription Channel Type** and make a note of the **Communication Subscription Channel Type ID** from the URL.  
(It starts with **0eB**.)

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*5hFt_TOF3t6_4aX0)

Next, enter the values using the following example as reference.

① **CommunicationSubscriptionChannelType**  
0eBHo0000000LZqMAM （← ※ your own value）

② **ConsentCapturedDateTime**  
Running Flow Interview > CurrentDateTime

③ **ConsentCapturedSourceDetails**  
No configuration required

④ **ConsentCapturedSourceName**  
Record-Triggered Flow

⑤ **ConsentCapturedSourceType**  
Flow Action

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*ZKOuI87ak4JRJUF0)

Next, for **ConsentId**, you need to configure a **Formula**, so select **New Resource**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*DhmF-QjBXsBMMWNy)

For the resource type, select **Formula**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*tLBEjQCc4KD6iNDx)

After deciding on any API Reference Name (example: `consentId`), set the data type to **Text** and enter the following formula.

The ID beginning with **“0eB”** here should also use the **Communication Subscription Channel Type ID** that you noted earlier.

For the `{!$Record.Email}` part, it is sufficient as long as the email address that triggered this flow can ultimately be retrieved.

⑥ **ConsentId**  
{!$Record.Email}&”#”&”0eBHo0000000LZqMAM”

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:853/0*ul_2WU2j6nDXaTqa)

For the remaining values, enter the following.

⑦ **ConsentStatus**  
OPT\_IN

⑧ **ContactPointValue**  
Contact from Get Individual > Email

⑨ **Name**  
No configuration

⑩ **PartyId**  
No configuration

⑪ **UpdatedTime**  
Running Flow Interview > CurrentDateTime

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*QQavl4EfEjLIS5Oy)

That’s all. I strongly recommend **thoroughly testing everything before deploying to production**.

## What did you think?

**“Consent Creation”** is one of the most heavily discussed topics in Marketing Cloud Next.

In reality, there is no single universally correct answer. The design and implementation need to be adjusted according to each company’s consent management policies, data model, and operational requirements.

Therefore, rather than treating the contents of this article as the “one and only best practice”, I hope you will view it simply as one example of a feature usage and implementation pattern.

This time, I introduced several automation patterns for consent creation, but in actual production environments, it is important to carefully design aspects such as the division of responsibilities with Data Cloud, retry handling, and duplicate creation prevention.

## Query for Checking Consent Status

Finally, as a bonus, here is a query that allows you to easily verify in **Query Editor** whether consent records have actually been created.

It should be useful during testing.

SELECT ContactPointValue, Status, LastModifiedDate, SourceType, SourceName  
FROM (  
    SELECT  
        a.ssot\_\_ContactPointValueText\_\_c AS ContactPointValue,  
        a.ssot\_\_ConsentStatus\_\_c AS Status,  
        a.ssot\_\_LastModifiedDate\_\_c + interval   
        a.ssot\_\_ConsentCapturedSourceType\_\_c AS SourceType,  
        a.ssot\_\_ConsentCapturedSourceName\_\_c AS SourceName  
    FROM ssot\_\_CommunicationSubscriptionConsent\_\_dlm a  
    JOIN ssot\_\_CommunicationSubscriptionChannelType\_\_dlm b   
        ON a.ssot\_\_CommunicationSubscriptionChannelTypeId\_\_c = b.ssot\_\_Id\_\_c  
    -- WHERE a.ssot\_\_ContactPointValueText\_\_c =   
) sub  
ORDER BY LastModifiedDate DESC   
LIMIT 50

After an audience reaches the **“Create Consent”** element, it may take approximately 5 minutes before the actual consent record is created.

There can be a slight delay, so during testing, please wait a little before checking the results.

That’s all for this time.

Stay tuned for more Salesforce Marketing Cloud tips! 😊

## Nobuyuki Watanabe

[https://www.linkedin.com/in/nobuyuki-watanabe/](https://www.linkedin.com/in/nobuyuki-watanabe/) (+Follow me)

title: SFMC Tips #150 : Marketing Cloud Next: Custom Event-Triggered Flow
markdown:
[

![Nobuyuki Watanabe @marketingcloudtips](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fill:40:40/1*zLRnWqPVgEgwGDwBBiwV3w.png)



](/@marketingcloudtips?source=post_page---byline--7ae54b7798b5---------------------------------------)

7 min read

Jul 17, 2025

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*DesT_S3_KcGN5aEZ)

Photo by [Jordan Whitfield](https://unsplash.com/@whitfieldjordan?utm_source=medium&utm_medium=referral) on [Unsplash](https://unsplash.com/?utm_source=medium&utm_medium=referral)

With the Summer ’25 new feature release of Marketing Cloud Next Growth & Advanced Editions, it became possible to create custom event-triggered flows by leveraging Salesforce Personalization **“Engagement Signals”.** This makes it possible to trigger flows when configured conditions are met.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*jU49gc5J6OL3AZnC)

Standard event-triggered flows that existed previously include actions such as email opens and link clicks. However, with the newly available custom event-triggered flows, the following use cases can now be considered.

- Create a ToDo when a text link on a website is clicked
- Send a company introduction email when a PDF download button is clicked
- Send an order confirmation email when a purchase is completed
- Send a reservation confirmation email when a reservation is completed

**Examples of signals provided by Salesforce**:

- [Product View](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=mktg.persnl_es_examples_prod_view_engmt.htm&language=en_US&type=5)
- [Product Click](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=mktg.persnl_es_examples_prod_click_engmt.htm&language=en_US&type=5)
- [Add To Cart](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=mktg.persnl_es_examples_add_to_cart_engmt.htm&language=en_US&type=5)
- [Product Order](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=mktg.persnl_es_examples_product_order_engmt.htm&language=en_US&type=5)
- [Knowledge Article Click](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=mktg.persnl_es_examples_article_click_engmt.htm&language=en_US&type=5)

## Considerations When Configuring Signal Definitions

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*ahWiP9DpqxlTYsOd)

- With the Summer ’26 new feature release, when selecting DMOs used for signals, **not only Engagement DMOs but also Profile DMOs can now be selected.**
- In Salesforce Personalization itself, signals are designed so that, in addition to the fields of the selected Engagement DMO (Primary DMO), fields from all related DMOs (Secondary DMOs) can also be used in conditions.
- However, to use this in event-triggered flows within Marketing Cloud Growth & Advanced Editions, it is necessary to check **“Enable this engagement signal for use in flows”.** In this case, only fields from the Primary Engagement DMO become available.
- This has been improved in the Summer ’26 new feature release, allowing **one additional related DMO** to be added. Related DMOs can only be configured when the relationship between the Primary DMO and Secondary DMO is **Many:1 or 1:1. (1:Many relationships are not supported.)**
- Up to **10 fields** from the added related DMO can be used in signal definitions and filter conditions.
- For example, it is now possible to select the Website Engagement DMO (Primary DMO) together with the Individual DMO (Secondary DMO), trigger a flow when a document request PDF is downloaded, and reference the customer’s Title field so that emails are triggered only for people with specific job titles.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*9r935uYRGIDz0yQ3)

## Configuration Steps

1\. Select the “Engagement Signals” tab and click “New”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*VMVXTt0PNl5ueLwD)

2\. To make the engagement signal available for use in flows, make sure to check this checkbox. Select the DMO for the target engagement category and click “Next”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*GRELo0LbiAnwydap)

_Note: As mentioned earlier, checking this checkbox limits available fields from related DMOs. However, when using this in Marketing Cloud Next,_ **_enabling the checkbox is mandatory._**

3\. On the next page, in this example the Web Engagement DMO is selected, resulting in the following input example.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*Fr7z79G9PpmXiSMI)

- **User Identifier:** Individual
- **Timestamp Identifier:** Engagement Date Time
- **Item Identifier:** Blank
- **Event Identifier:** Website Engagement ID

Item Identifier is an identifier used by the signal to better distinguish engagement activity. For example, a specific “Product SKU” used to identify a particular engagement.

Event Identifier allows grouping of the same engagement and exclusion of duplicate records.

Reference: If **“Enable this engagement signal for use in flows”** is not checked, the next screen requires configuration of how events are counted.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*8u7W39CwEghMb0RI)

**Option Descriptions**

- **Count events individually (default)  
  **Even if the same user performs the same action multiple times, each occurrence is recorded as a separate event.  
  Example: If the same person clicks the same email multiple times, each click is counted separately and reflected in the total click count.
- **Count repeated events together (using additional fields)  
  **By grouping events using specific conditions (such as User ID or bulk email send ID), events can be counted as a single grouped event. Up to **three fields** can be added.  
  Example: Even if one user clicks multiple times within the same email, those clicks can be counted as a single click event. (For example, grouped by User ID + Email ID + Event Type.)

4\. Next, define filters in order to better identify specific engagement activity.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*ECN1gIQPhMVSgCCI)

For example, it is possible to trigger only when there is a history of viewing a specific web page. In this example, the trigger is configured when the page **“Product\_Pricing\_Page”** is viewed.

## Get Nobuyuki Watanabe @marketingcloudtips’s stories in your inbox

Join Medium for free to get updates from this writer.

Remember me for faster sign in

**Operators Available for Filters**

- For both text fields and date fields, the only available operators are:  
  **“Equals (Not Equals is unavailable for date fields) / Has Value / No Value”.**
- For numeric fields (including currency), in addition to:  
  **“Equals (Not Equals) / Has Value / No Value”,  
  **the following are also available:  
  **“Less Than / Less Than or Equal / Greater Than or Equal / Greater Than”.**

**Filter Conditions for Text Fields**

- **English uppercase and lowercase characters are treated as case-sensitive exact matches.**
- Japanese values such as “商品価格表” also trigger successfully.
- If spaces exist between words such as **“Product Pricing Page”, the trigger will not work.** Use a single-word format such as **“Product\_Pricing\_Page”.** “Product-Pricing-Page” is not treated as a single word.

_Although AND conditions and OR conditions can be configured,_ **_complex combinations mixing AND and OR conditions are not supported._** _If using AND conditions, all conditions become AND. If using OR conditions, all conditions become OR._

5\. Finally, decide the signal name and save to complete the configuration.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*Jh1Cacj6AjZVnDOB)

**Editing Restrictions and Deletion Method**

- **Once a signal is created, its definition and filters cannot be edited.** To delete and recreate it, the related flow must first be deactivated, and then the flow itself must also be deleted.

6\. Confirmed that it also appears in the Event Library of event-triggered flows.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*jwpDmMLDutvMn_DD)

7\. Create and execute a flow that actually creates a ToDo.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*QDJ22Hj66M4bPCL0)

8\. When the Product Pricing Table link on the landing page was clicked, a ToDo indicating that the existing lead clicked the pricing table was created after approximately **2 to 15 minutes.** Success.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*25WbXkNSainPLrsX)

**About Trigger Time Lag**

- Normally, there is a delay of approximately **2 to 15 minutes** between the occurrence of the event and the trigger execution. The relevant engagement data must first be stored in the Engagement DMO.
- In rare cases, it may not trigger within 2 to 15 minutes and may appear stuck, then suddenly trigger several hours later.

**About the Email Address Used for Sending**

Regarding this sending email address, the Spring ’26 new feature release introduced the ability to specify the source used for retrieving contact points.

- ① If included in the Primary DMO, retrieve directly from the event data
- ② Retrieve from the Data Graph

_※ In case ② is selected,_ **_emails are sent to all email addresses linked to the Unified Individual. This behavior is by design._**

The flow automatically retrieves contact points based on the selected method. This ensures that messaging actions are executed reliably, even for newly created person records or records currently under data processing.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*_8qJBBVzQWciG_BF)

**More Detailed Event Trigger Configuration Available (Summer ’26 and Later)**

With the Summer ’26 new feature release, it became possible to configure more detailed trigger conditions using fields from the Primary DMO. To configure trigger conditions, select **“Add Condition”** in the **“Additional Criteria”** section of the Start element.

Additionally, by defining re-entry conditions, it is now possible to flexibly control whether the same user can enter the flow again.

The following controls are available:

- **Always**: Allow re-entry every time the event occurs
- **After completion**: Allow re-entry only after the flow completes
- **Never**: Execute only once (re-entry not allowed)

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*HBYlWhLQ3fo2_4HY)

## Conclusion

This is just my personal interpretation, but because the term “**Engagement Signals**” sounds somewhat unfamiliar, it may feel like a completely new and special feature. However, in reality, **since Data Cloud-triggered flows do not include major marketing-related elements such as “Send Email Message,” it becomes much easier to understand when viewed simply as a mechanism that compensates for those missing capabilities through another flow**.

Fundamentally, the concept is simply that when data enters a DMO, records matching configured conditions are triggered.

At first, it may feel slightly difficult to understand, but by actually working with it, you will gradually develop a sense of:

- what types of scenarios it should be used for
- how to design it in a manageable way

My recommendation is to start with small experiments and gradually become familiar with it.

That’s all for this time.

Stay tuned for more Salesforce Marketing Cloud tips! 😊

## Nobuyuki Watanabe

[https://www.linkedin.com/in/nobuyuki-watanabe/](https://www.linkedin.com/in/nobuyuki-watanabe/) (+Follow me)

title: SFMC Tips #151 : Marketing Cloud Next: Setup Steps for SDO (Demo)
markdown:
[

![Nobuyuki Watanabe @marketingcloudtips](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fill:40:40/1*zLRnWqPVgEgwGDwBBiwV3w.png)



](/@marketingcloudtips?source=post_page---byline--be441f7c37d8---------------------------------------)

10 min read

Jul 22, 2025

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*qeJqejE14-dJCYhL)

Photo by [Reuben Rohard](https://unsplash.com/@reubenrohard?utm_source=medium&utm_medium=referral) on [Unsplash](https://unsplash.com/?utm_source=medium&utm_medium=referral)

Salesforce provides an environment called a **Simple Demo Org (SDO)** for users who belong to Salesforce partner companies for evaluation and demonstration purposes. An SDO can be requested on an individual basis through **Partner Learning Camp (PLC)**, making it relatively easy to get started.

By using an SDO, you can actually operate **Marketing Cloud Next** features while conducting functional validation, demonstrations, and even exploring use cases and learning the platform.

In this article, we will introduce how to obtain an **SDO and the basic setup procedures** required to use Marketing Cloud Next.

## A Free Development Environment Is Now Available for General Users

With the release of a new [Trailhead](https://trailhead.salesforce.com/content/learn/modules/marketing-cloud-next-email-sending-essentials) at the end of May 2026, a free development environment for Marketing Cloud Next has also been made available to general users.

Please refer to the following article.

## How to Extend an SDO

An SDO can be requested on an individual basis rather than a company basis, and there is no limit to the number of requests. The initial validity period is 30 days, but it can be extended for up to one year.

If, after gaining access, you feel that you are likely to continue using it, we recommend submitting an extension request as early as possible. Once the expiration date passes, it may become impossible to retrieve the organization information, making it difficult to reapply. We recommend noting down your Organization ID in advance.

### Extension Procedure

1. [Log in to Partner Community](https://partners.salesforce.com/pdx/s/) and launch **Ask Agentforce** from the bottom-right corner of the screen.
2. Click **Extend a Trial Org**.
3. Enter the **Organization ID** of the SDO you would like to extend. You can find the Organization ID under **Setup > Company Information**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*GI72GyNnDmj0nZbM)

## Getting Started with Marketing Cloud Engagement+

Since March 2026, the Marketing Cloud Engagement demo environments provided to Salesforce partners have been automatically migrated to Marketing Cloud Engagement+.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*7uL4BQqgbL42YZ57)

Send customers from a Flow to a Journey using the Send to Journey action

While some functionality is already available, not all features are currently enabled. When performing testing and validation, it is recommended to assume that certain features may not yet be available.

## Email Sending from Flows Is Available in SDO Orgs

Since March 2026, email sending has become available because the **Default From Address** under **Unified Messaging > Email > Settings** is enabled.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*tE9PTKGiyJwOd4t2)

In environments where this setting is enabled, you can use sender addresses such as:

- Example: **\[No Reply: no-reply@cdp2.mx.salesforce.com\]**

as the From Address.

### Other Considerations

- Landing Pages and Forms are also available.
- SMS and WhatsApp are not available.

## **Setup Procedure**

### **How to Obtain an SDO**

1\. Go to [**Partner Learning Camp (PLC)**](https://partnerlearningcamp.salesforce.com/s/learner-dashboard) and click on the **“Demo Org”** tab.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*a_MkXbag8GwKoBKX)

2\. Next, select **SDO** as the **Demo Type**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*w1aE2ZdJx44MPAcs)

3\. The **Username (Login ID)** will be filled in automatically. You can leave it as is — it’s typically helpful since it includes the creation date of the SDO, making it easy to tell when it was generated. Check the agreement box and click **Submit**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*du2eICl8iEpg08ZT)

4\. After clicking **Submit**, you’ll receive around 20 to 30 emails within approximately 6 hours. Among them, the only important one is the **“Welcome to Salesforce: Verify your account”** email, usually sent as the 2nd or 3rd message. Be sure to save that email and feel free to delete the rest.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*x-2F2EKZt1i6xODa)

5\. The contents of this email include the following:

- **Top section (Verify Account)**: Password reset link (you’ll need to complete this only the first time).
- **Middle section**: The URL to access your org (be sure to bookmark it).
- **Bottom section**: Your **Username (Login ID)** (make sure to save it).

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:799/0*c5NMZehNFMPosCBX)

6\. You now have access to your Salesforce SDO. At this stage, **Marketing Cloud is not yet available**, so try navigating to **Sales Cloud** or another app from the App Launcher first.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*XV8trUx8W0TfT7pH)

### **Assigning Permission Sets**

1\. From **Setup**, go to **Users**, and in the **View** dropdown, select **All Users**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*YoBJ7MImY-fyYRzT)

2\. Click on **your own** **user name**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*ukBDB4cgYw2e09GQ)

3\. Select **Permission Set Assignments**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*m-IMBdd2Tnz7RY_u)

4\. Click on **Edit Assignments**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*SMlvk1LP8mtvjwLp)

Add the following five permission sets:

- **Data Cloud Architect**
- **Marketing Cloud Admin**
- **Tableau Next Included App Business User**
- **View Unified Engagement History Dashboard (Spring ’26 onward)**
- **Send Distributed Marketing Messages (Spring ’26 onward)**

Once added, click **Save**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/1*1bU3Sg3SBi0ZpwJn2fO2TA.png)

### **Data Cloud Setup**

1\. Let’s start by setting up **Data Cloud**. Click the gear icon in the upper-right corner and select **Data Cloud Setup**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*4pvxrQwXDFkwEUjI)

2\. You’ll be taken to the setup screen. Scroll all the way to the bottom and click **Get Started**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*4Ou-Kka6tq_rE2Z7)

3\. The initial setup of Data Cloud is very simple — just one click, and it will start configuring automatically. This process takes about **one hour**, so please wait patiently.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*f8bTY_2Y5O81ZBHM)

4\. No confirmation email will be sent after setup is complete. After about one hour, return to this screen to verify the setup. You’ll see a screen like the one below, indicating completion.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*HB3l9FVNoAFcR4ga)

### **Marketing Cloud Setup**

1\. Next, go to the regular **Setup** page, search for **“Marketing Cloud”**, and click on **Basic Settings**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*G8EXZba3reSalvH2)

2\. In the SDO, you should see that **Marketing Cloud is already enabled**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*8Yi_Iuze7HQlH_qn)

3\. Scroll down and click **Update** to install the **Data Kit**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*tm54uz8FV6iqPK62)

4\. The installation will begin and may take around **30 minutes** to complete.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*jt7bZcLsS3l7vDrn)

5\. Installation errors are quite common, so if an error occurs, please retry the installation several times.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*b1Nfy2GCs_hc5Oqz)

\*If the error still persists, please check the following known issues.

### Known Issues and Workarounds for Marketing **Data Kit**

In SDOs or Sandbox environments, the following error may appear:  
**“A required package is missing. Package “Salesforce Standard Data Model”, Version x or later must be installed first.”**

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*qCw4if9sGXOuNFMX)

This may prevent the Data Kit from being deployed. In such cases, please refer to the [help documentation](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=002234049&type=1) below and install the latest version of the Salesforce Standard Data Model.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/1*qUZTpYqY-tT9gSKyYaS70w.png)

**Data Cloud: Resolve “Salesforce Data Model Version x.xx or later must be installed first” error.**

### **Identity Resolution (Creating Unified Individuals)**

1\. Scroll down slightly and you’ll find the section for configuring **Identity Resolution**. Click the **“Generate Ruleset”** button.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*mCF-XPHfSBbqGWYc)

2\. The status will change to **“In Progress”.**

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*7RRUROIqaU8YMwld)

3\. After about **5 minutes**, try refreshing the page. The status should now show as **“Success”.**

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*C6vvQcl-b1DE8m3p)

If you receive the error message:

> “We couldn’t generate a ruleset because your org is missing the required Party Identification DMO. Set up the Party Identification DMO and try again.”

you can avoid this error by creating it from the **New** button under the **Identity Resolution** tab in Data 360. When created from there, the error does not occur.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*9fS_gAkIwOtVs9VQ)

4\. Open the **App Launcher** and search for **“Marketing”.** You will see two versions — select the one with the **white icon**, which corresponds to **Marketing Cloud Advanced Edition**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*5ExlwfQRujbm8K-J)

5\. This will take you to the **Marketing Cloud homepage**. From there, click on **Identity Resolution**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*g_kvWrx2b0MLtDKH)

6\. The previously created **Ruleset** should appear. Click on **“Individual Identity Resolution”.**

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*6MxWzljJK8LUzLpX)

7\. A **warning message** may be displayed, but you can proceed with the identity resolution. Click **“Run Ruleset”.**

## Get Nobuyuki Watanabe @marketingcloudtips’s stories in your inbox

Join Medium for free to get updates from this writer.

Remember me for faster sign in

_Important:_ After clicking, it may take around 10 seconds before any response is visible — **do not click multiple times.**

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*tlh8JyBaJUiiEOlA)

8\. A green banner saying **“Your ruleset job is running.”** will appear.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*3ZD0LfKDBZf0rNr4)

9\. After about **10 minutes**, refresh the page. Identity Resolution should be completed, and you’ll see the number of **Unified Individual IDs** that have been created.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*8cVEUw4OVAqAGk4v)

_Note:_ You can only run a **Ruleset up to 4 times within a 24-hour period**, so for any additional runs, follow the same manual execution steps as outlined above.

### **Registering Your Organization’s Physical Address**

1\. Go to **Setup**, then navigate to **Channels > Email**, and click on **“Go to Company Information”.**

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*t1z8gcx3wnDUmDcf)

2\. Click the **Edit** button.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*S-9qLo7edTijcgvs)

3\. Enter your company’s **physical address**. Since this is a demo organization, you can enter any placeholder information.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*rWaXSVJcbscMymuq)

### **Enabling Einstein Features**

1\. Still under **Channels > Email**, scroll all the way to the bottom of the page.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*vgpwJr5d3CnqEXjT)

2\. These are **optional setup**, but it’s recommended to enable each of the **Einstein features** for testing purposes.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*ZrRDnfaUJmbYgBNB)

### Reference: Common Pitfall When Viewing the SDO Landing Page

When clicking the public URL of a landing page, you may encounter an error saying “**URL No Longer Exists**”. This error is typically caused by **login IP address restrictions**. You can resolve this issue by following the steps below:

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*FggQ094-Hw2Ec4fx)

1\. Go to **Settings > Digital Experiences > All Sites**, and click the **Builder** for **Marketing Landing Pages**.

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*XrJVUgw8HBlBHZ7h)

2\. From the left-hand menu, click the gear icon, then go to **General > Guest User Profile**, and click the link for **Marketing Landing Pages Profile**.

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*5UJmS9NkaBfIs_ou)

3\. Select **System > Login IP Ranges**.

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*zXER0MpT03Sl3-DH)

4\. Delete the currently configured IP restrictions.

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*rcslVkL4jGK-MtSm)

After completing these steps, the landing page should be viewable. Please give it a try.

title: SFMC Tips #153 : Marketing Cloud Next: Leveraging Prospects
markdown:
[

![Nobuyuki Watanabe @marketingcloudtips](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fill:40:40/1*zLRnWqPVgEgwGDwBBiwV3w.png)



](/@marketingcloudtips?source=post_page---byline--8aa701aea983---------------------------------------)

7 min read

Aug 5, 2025

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*qB6Zu_xHtbQd8zSj)

Photo by [Sri Gowda](https://unsplash.com/@sri_go?utm_source=medium&utm_medium=referral) on [Unsplash](https://unsplash.com/?utm_source=medium&utm_medium=referral)

In Marketing Cloud Next (Marketing Cloud Growth & Advanced Edition), marketers can leverage “prospects” to ensure that only highly qualified leads are passed on to sales representatives.

A “prospect” refers to a potential customer who has not yet reached the deal stage but has provided contact information (such as an email address) through channels like sign-up forms. At this point, they have not been assigned to a sales representative.

On the other hand, a “lead” is a prospect who has met certain criteria, such as scoring thresholds through marketing efforts, and has been assigned to a sales representative. Furthermore, a “contact” represents a lead that has progressed to the deal stage and is moving toward an actual transaction.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*cJJ_6tY0CD-VO8L-)

### **People Status**

- **Prospect:** Status before being assigned to a sales representative
- **Lead:** Status after being assigned to a sales representative
- **Contact:** Status when the lead has converted into a deal and the transaction has started

### **Deal Conversion Likelihood**

- **Prospect:** Low to Medium (Handled by: Marketer)
- **Lead:** Medium to High (Handled by: Sales Representative & Marketer)

## **Steps for Adding and Importing Prospects**

When you have individuals who are not yet qualified as leads but may have a potential to purchase in the future, you can add them as “prospects” in Marketing Cloud.

> **_Note:_** _Simply adding a prospect does not enable marketing communications to them. To send marketing communications, you must create a_ **_Consent Record_** _for each channel._

### Methods for Adding Prospects

1. Adding Prospects via Event Trigger Flow
2. Manually Adding a Single Prospect
3. Bulk Adding Prospects via CSV Import

### 1. Adding Prospects via Event Trigger Flow

You can create prospects through a sign-up form by using an Event Trigger Flow when the form is submitted.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:764/0*CaERZORnVT08WGOw.png)

> **_Note:_** _When using a standard sign-up form template, elements for lead creation are automatically generated. Therefore, if you wish to create a prospect instead of a lead, you’ll need to manually adjust and modify the form settings._

For detailed steps on creating a sign-up form, please refer to the article below:

### 2. Manually Adding a Single Prospect

You can register prospects one by one directly from the Marketing Cloud interface.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*WXe4YIpwxEF3s0Gl)

1. Navigate to the **Prospects** tab.
2. Click the **New** button in the top right corner.
3. Fill in the prospect’s information in the form.

**Required Fields:** To enable sending communications, at least one of the following must be provided — _Last Name_, _Email Address_, or _Phone Number_. Additionally, _Currency_ is also a mandatory field.

4\. Once all required fields are filled out, click **Save** to complete the registration.

### 3. Bulk Adding Prospects via CSV Import

When you need to register multiple prospects at once, you can perform a bulk import using a CSV file.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*0HFKZtqQ6Ss3-wbl)

1. Navigate to the **Prospects** tab and click **Import** in the top right corner.
2. Select **Import from File**, then click **Next**.
3. Click **Upload File**, select your CSV file, and click **Next**.
4. Map each column in the CSV file to the corresponding fields in Salesforce.
5. After completing the field mapping, click **Start Import**.
6. Click **Finish** to complete the process.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*mAVBDV02QOephrhM)

## Converting Prospects

Prospects can be converted into Leads or Contacts either manually or through automated flows.

> **_Tip:_** _With the Summer ’25 release, it is now possible to convert Prospects directly into_ **_Contacts_**_._

### Methods for Converting Prospects

1. Manually convert a Prospect to a Lead
2. Manually convert a Prospect to a Contact
3. Automatically convert Prospects to Leads or Contacts via Trigger Flows

### 1. Manually Converting a Prospect to a Lead

Identify prospects who meet the engagement score criteria and convert them into Leads.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*fRTKk98HZv1LU5cf)

1. Navigate to the **Prospects** tab, select the target Prospect, and open the record page.
2. Click **Convert** in the top right corner.
3. Choose whether to create a **new Lead** or select an **existing Lead**.

- If converting to an existing Lead, only the empty fields in the Lead record will be updated with the Prospect’s information.

4\. Enter required information such as Record Owner, Company Name, etc.

## Get Nobuyuki Watanabe @marketingcloudtips’s stories in your inbox

Join Medium for free to get updates from this writer.

Remember me for faster sign in

5\. Click **Convert**.

- The entered information will be mapped to the Lead record accordingly.

> **_Important:_** _If there are any_ **_custom required fields_** _on the Lead object, you will not be able to convert the Prospect until those fields are addressed._

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*kCN6TzRQMp8Wih0a)

### 2. Manually Converting a Prospect to a Contact

Identify qualified Prospects and convert them into Contacts. A Contact can be associated with a **new Account** or an **existing Account**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*Ew9uvM7WneT4MxC5)

1. Navigate to the **Prospects** tab, select the target Prospect, and open the record page.
2. Click **Convert**.
3. In the **Convert Prospect** window, select **Contact**.
4. Enter or select the related **Contact** and **Account**.

- If you select an existing Contact, you cannot create a new Account during the conversion.

5\. (Optional) Associate the Contact with a **new Opportunity** or an **existing Opportunity**.

- If you do not wish to create an Opportunity, select **Don’t create an opportunity upon conversion**.

6\. Assign a Record Owner for the Contact and click **Convert**.

> **_Important:_** _If there are_ **_custom required fields_** _on the Contact object, you will not be able to convert the Prospect until those fields are addressed._

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*Fwr3KLJYrCK_BgTu)

### 3. Automatically Converting Prospects via Trigger Flows

You can automate the conversion of Prospects to Leads or Contacts using **Data Cloud Trigger Flows**, **Event Trigger Flows**, or **Form Trigger Flows**.

The detailed setup process for automating these conversions is explained in the following article:

## Setting Up Lead Assignment Rules

Once a prospect is converted into a Lead, the **Lead Assignment Rules** determine the record owner automatically.

> _If you need to assign different owners based on specific lead criteria, you’ll need to create new assignment rules manually._

Navigate to **Setup**, search for **Lead Assignment Rules** in the Quick Find box, and select **Lead Assignment Rules** to create a new rule.

### Examples of Lead Assignment Rules

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*nldMyCzPKzU5r6Zp)

✅ **Rule 1: NOT(ISNEW())**  
**Condition:** The record is not newly created (i.e., an existing lead is being updated).  
**Assignee:** Current Owner (Same User)  
**Purpose:** Ensure that ownership remains unchanged when editing existing leads.

✅ **Rule 2: Lead: State/Province equals CA**  
**Condition:** The State/Province field is set to “CA” (California).  
**Assignee:** Queue “Sales — Leads West”  
**Purpose:** Assign leads from the West Coast to the dedicated Sales — Leads West queue.

✅ **Rule 3: Lead: Lead Source equals Website**  
**Condition:** The Lead Source field is “Website”.  
**Assignee:** Queue “Sales — Lead”  
**Purpose:** Route leads that came through the website to a specific sales team.

✅ **Rule 4: Lead: Email not equal to null**  
**Condition:** The Email field is populated.  
**Assignee:** Queue “Sales — Lead”  
**Email Notification:** Enabled  
**Purpose:** Assign leads with an email address to the Sales — Lead queue and notify the assigned owner.

## Considerations When Working with Prospects

When managing **Prospects** in Marketing Cloud, please keep the following points in mind:

### 1. Converted Prospects Do Not Appear in List Views

- Once a Prospect is converted to a **Lead**, it will no longer appear in List Views.
- However, converted Prospects remain visible in **Data Explorer**.
- Even if you apply a filter like **Converted = True**, they will not be displayed in the List View.

### 2. Exclude Converted Prospects When Building Segments

- When creating a segment of Prospects, you must explicitly exclude converted records.
- For example, add a condition such as **Prospect Status != Converted**.
- Otherwise, converted Prospects will be included in your segment by default.

### 3. Prospects Cannot Be Added to Campaigns

- Only **Leads** and **Contacts** can be added as Campaign Members.
- Prospects are not eligible to be directly added to Campaigns.

### 4. Limitations on Prospect Management

- Prospects cannot be imported using the **Data Import Wizard**.
- Prospects cannot be copied or mass deleted.

### 5. Attribute Carryover After Conversion

- After a prospect is converted, the engagement score and engagement history (such as Email and Web) are carried over as long as the unified ID remains the same (following ID resolution and the update of precomputed insights).

### 6. Maintaining Relationships Between Prospects and Leads

- The relationship between a converted Prospect and its corresponding Lead is preserved through:
- The **Converted Lead** field on the Prospect object, which stores the Lead ID.
- A related record created in the **Identity Match DMO (Data Model Object)** to maintain the linkage.

## Conclusion

If your sales team can adequately handle potential customers before they reach the deal stage, there may not be a pressing need to utilize Prospects. However, if the volume of potential customers becomes too large to manage effectively, leveraging Prospects is highly recommended to improve sales efficiency.

By combining **Marketing Engagement Scores** with automated **Flows**, you can seamlessly pass only highly qualified Prospects to the sales team, enabling more precise and efficient collaboration between marketing and sales.

That’s all for now.

Stay tuned for more Salesforce Marketing Cloud tips! 😊

## Nobuyuki Watanabe

[https://www.linkedin.com/in/nobuyuki-watanabe/](https://www.linkedin.com/in/nobuyuki-watanabe/) (+Follow me)

title: SFMC Tips #154 : Marketing Cloud Next: Converting Prospects via Flow
markdown:
## Converting Prospects to Leads or Contacts Using Data Cloud Trigger Flow

In this example, I will convert prospects who meet specific conditions in the **Marketing Engagement Score** (a Calculated Insight Object in Data Cloud) into Leads or Contacts using a Data Cloud Trigger Flow.

### Configuration Steps

1. Create a **New Flow** and select **Data Cloud-Trigger Flow**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*tq4hjZSrQdDgd_Qq)

2\. In the **Start** element settings, select **Marketing Engagement Score** from the object dropdown.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*MUqq3zSEKpzX0jp1)

3\. For the trigger condition, choose **A record is created or updated**. In the condition requirements dropdown, select **All Conditions Are Met (AND)** and set the following condition:

- **Engagement\_Score\_\_c** is greater than or equal to **50**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*U7hLMx_kaA8o_qBp)

4\. Next, add a **Get Records** element to retrieve data from the Data Cloud object **Unified Link Individual**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*PykaP8uAKemyEHvl)

5\. For the condition, on the left side select **Unified Individual ID** from the **Unified Link Individual** object, and on the right side select **Triggering …**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*kNTpKYxb4i8Ux-bU)

6\. Continue by selecting **Unified\_Individual** from **Triggering …** and leave other settings as default.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/1*8ewP-XaGsZrkbBvO_wTVsQ.png)

7\. Add another **Get Records** element to retrieve records from the Salesforce **Prospect** object.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*bPOYZ8ifxy0HUWqd)

8\. On the left, choose **Prospect ID**, and on the right, select the **Unified Link Individual from Get …** resource from the **Get Records** action.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*JlsYloLEN0bpYDtu)

9\. On the right side, select **Individual Id**. Leave other settings as default.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*MJa0jhSpwKyfXO6C)

10\. Next, add a **Decision** element to determine whether the contact in the flow is a **Prospect**, and create a branch accordingly. For the first condition, select **Unified Link Individual from Get …** from the **Get Records** resource on the left.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*pQg5Mi5DxkWnjVoW)

11\. Choose **Data Source Object**. (Be careful not to select **Data Source**.)

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*nIH3QlHpdfhxA7Do)

12\. On the right side, input **Prospect**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*fs1LJm8nr4ZG2LP4)

13\. For the second condition, select **Prospect from Get …** from the **Get Records** resource.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*a7pAOt4JbZV1dVAk)

14\. On the left, select **Prospect Status** from the picklist.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*3WBJ9xhb5_xGiCWq)

15\. Change the operator to **Does Not Equal**, and on the right side, select **Converted**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*fsB4QZ7s6OZsUy_0)

16\. Finally, on the **Prospect** path, add an **Action** element. From the list, select the **Convert Prospect** action.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*exZMfhE9aHW6r0CK)

17\. Set the **Label** and **API Name** for the action. For **Prospect ID**, select **Prospect from Get …** from the **Get Records** resource.

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:856/0*KpuCf49ZVdCfoos5)

18\. For the field, select **Prospect Id**.

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:874/0*Mn5_7Tku7ZVy2Lg1)

19\. In **Converted Status**, enter **Converted**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*VYFiKr2ZIjt8o4kU)

20\. Choose whether to update an existing Lead or create a new Lead upon conversion.

- If you want to create a **new Lead**, toggle ON the option. The input field that appears afterward can be left blank.
- If you want to convert to an **existing Lead**, only empty fields in the existing Lead will be updated with values from the Prospect.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*k3nbVeDCBxS_XefF)

21\. Finally, save the flow and click **Activate** to complete.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*LvZC-p-XEkd5-u2Q)

## Conclusion

In this article, I introduced an example using a **Data Cloud-Trigger Flow**, but it’s also possible to perform lead conversion using a **Record-Triggered Flow** when the Prospect Status is updated to **“Qualified”**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*BpAAOreDO_DP6sp2)

Additionally, by leveraging a **Form Trigger Flow**, you can check whether a Prospect record already exists during a signup form campaign. This helps prevent unnecessary creation of duplicate Leads by converting existing Prospects into Leads in a controlled manner.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*avpvZUQfRnSPgLTc)

By choosing the appropriate **Flow Trigger Type** based on the situation, you can build more flexible automations. Be sure to actively explore and make the most out of Flows in your processes!
