Also remember that changing the condition of data space filter also becomes a trigger of incremental update. Well, if you think about it, this is also understandable.

## 9. Impact of credit consumption

Now, when full refresh occurs, credit consumption becomes very large.

Example calculation:

- Processing of 50,000 records → 5,000 credits are consumed

_※ Approximately 100,000 credits are consumed per 1 million records._

Let’s convert this with the default values of each edition of Marketing Cloud Next. Assume that there are 50,000 Contact records.

- In the case of Marketing Cloud Next Growth Edition  
  → Default 250,000 credits ⇒ all consumed in 50 times
- In the case of Marketing Cloud Next Advanced Edition  
  → Default 500,000 credits ⇒ all consumed in 100 times

If full refresh runs once a day,  
in the case of Growth Edition, credits will only last for 50 days.

You can intuitively understand that this is quite serious.

In the early stage of development, it is important to firmly understand how long the development environment will last and how many credits have been purchased.

## 10. Expected dangerous operation procedure

The following flow is very dangerous.

- First, execute Identity resolution with 50,000 linked records without thinking  
  ⇒ 5,000 credits consumed
- Add a new field to Individual DMO and execute Identity resolution again for now  
  ⇒ 5,000 credits consumed
- Since the rule set was inappropriate, correct it properly and execute Identity resolution again  
  ⇒ 5,000 credits consumed

With just this, 15,000 credits are consumed.  
This is tough…

## 11. What must be done in initial setup

In the initial phase, be sure to do the following.

**Mandatory check**

- Identity resolution auto run= OFF

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*gNreNlu2ru0_G3O4)

- Data stream automatic full refreash interval = None (no update)

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/1*H0-58W6l7P4jpzd1w5pEuw.png)

**Test policy**

- Narrow targets in advance with data space filter
- Verify with about 1,000 records (with conditions as unbiased as possible)  
  Example: “people whose address is XX prefecture”

**Things to do before full-scale Identity resolution**

- Completely determine fields to be linked from CRM to DSO and link them
- Complete all mappings from DLO to DMO
- Completely finalize rule set (Match Rules and Reconciliation Rules)

## Conclusion

As mentioned at the beginning, this content is organized for beginner Marketing Cloud Next users to first understand.

Therefore, in Data 360 operation in enterprise environments, different design concepts and considerations may be required, so please understand that point in advance when referring.

## Identity Resolution Principles

- Do not enable auto Identity resolution from the beginning
- Use data space filters effectively
- Accept that LastModifiedDate mapping cannot be removed
- Complete all field integrations to DSO at the start
- Complete all mappings to DMO
- Finalize rule sets before execution
- Do not perform full refresh on DSO after operations begin

title: SFMC Tips #276 : Marketing Cloud Next: How to Search Segments by Individual
SFMC Tips #277 : Marketing Cloud Next: Custom Preference Page Setup
markdown:
[

![Nobuyuki Watanabe @marketingcloudtips](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fill:40:40/1*zLRnWqPVgEgwGDwBBiwV3w.png)



](/@marketingcloudtips?source=post_page---byline--311bc587f406---------------------------------------)

4 min read

Apr 15, 2026

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*yD6TR20YPinNp8n6)

Photo by [Marie-Michèle Bouchard](https://unsplash.com/@minusculemarie?utm_source=medium&utm_medium=referral) on [Unsplash](https://unsplash.com/?utm_source=medium&utm_medium=referral)

In the [Spring ’26 release](/@marketingcloudtips/marketing-cloud-next-spring-26-release-highlights-24c0c804b0cb) of Marketing Cloud Next Growth & Advanced Edition, in addition to the standard preference page, it is now possible to create a **custom preference page** that matches your brand image, allowing customers to manage their subscriptions (opt-in/opt-out), such as email, by themselves.

- _It has been available in production environments since April 16, 2026!_

Previously, the standard preference page only allowed selecting which subscriptions to display, and no other changes could be made. However, by using a custom preference page, **it is now possible to provide a consistent and trustworthy experience aligned with your brand image**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/1*TGbUsp9ZO-YpZTLpmIsENQ.png)

In this article, I will explain the setup steps for this new feature, the custom preference page.

## Setup Steps

1\. In the settings screen, go to Marketing Cloud > Marketing Features, select “**Preference Page Customization**”, and confirm that the custom preference page is “**Enabled**”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*OqMqTTocbINvxklp)

2\. Click the “**Content**” tab, select the Marketing Cloud workspace, and click “Add — Content”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*ioYJxtAsxMM0fHDx)

3. Select “**Preference Page**” and start creating it.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*oybD0ZyCGNh-NfdP)

4\. Similar to a landing page, the Experience Builder screen will be displayed. Configure various settings here.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*bbhAd7R6gsh0AnGz)

**Considerations**

- **The button in the “Submit Buttons” component cannot be edited**
- **“Links” can be used**
- **“Merge fields” cannot be used**

5\. Use the “**Subscription List**” **component** to add channel types and the subscriptions you want to display.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/1*vn8cqTbcT99yXxFENhFhZw.png)

**Considerations**

- The customer’s **email address** is automatically populated in “**Contact Information**”

6\. After completing the setup, save and publish.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/1*8pQ4z58HV1bVclgaC8XJnw.png)

**7.** Go to the “**Consent**” tab, and from the action menu, set the created preference page as “**Set as Default**”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*s1Ce3ZFW3TjFHJaK)

8\. Set the default preference page for the current business unit.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*2wAfySH3PqgkoT-q)

9\. Create an email, include a link to the preference page, then save and publish it.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*tiJXzztxmCX003lA)

10\. If the following error occurs when publishing, the cause is that “the preference page is not set as default”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*M7emhMqRrrmSEhvv)

Displayed error message:  
_Something’s not right with your preference page. Your Salesforce admin can resolve the issue in Preference Manager._

## Get Nobuyuki Watanabe @marketingcloudtips’s stories in your inbox

Join Medium for free to get updates from this writer.

Remember me for faster sign in

11\. After publishing the email, send it.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*oqMZorD5PgIpkA_Y)

12\. Click the preference page link in the received email.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*KDQ75O5A9w-6xU2_)

13\. Confirm the display of the preference page.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/1*TGbUsp9ZO-YpZTLpmIsENQ.png)

**Note**

- This has been tested in an SDO org. If you encounter the error “**URL No Longer Exists**”, it is likely caused by “**Login IP Ranges**”. Please refer to the section “Common Pitfall When Viewing the SDO Landing Page” in [this article](/@marketingcloudtips/marketing-cloud-next-basic-setup-procedure-for-the-demo-environment-be441f7c37d8) and remove the restriction accordingly.

**Considerations**

- The display order of subscriptions is random
- It is currently not possible to fix the order (e.g., Brand A → Brand D)

title: SFMC Tips #280 : Marketing Cloud Next: Customize messages flexibly with AMPscript
markdown:
[

![Nobuyuki Watanabe @marketingcloudtips](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fill:40:40/1*zLRnWqPVgEgwGDwBBiwV3w.png)



](/@marketingcloudtips?source=post_page---byline--18272b5f6a1e---------------------------------------)

3 min read

Apr 22, 2026

1

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*D9CAYVnNI4ZHO-RC)

Photo by [Nathan Dumlao](https://unsplash.com/@nate_dumlao?utm_source=medium&utm_medium=referral) on [Unsplash](https://unsplash.com/?utm_source=medium&utm_medium=referral)

One of the biggest topics in the [Summer ’26 new feature release](/@marketingcloudtips/marketing-cloud-next-summer-26-release-highlights-04f6c5abdee6) for Marketing Cloud Next Growth & Advanced Editions is undoubtedly the **long-awaited support for AMPscript** (Marketing Cloud’s proprietary scripting language).

This makes it possible to achieve the same flexible message customization on Marketing Cloud Next as was previously available in Marketing Cloud Engagement. It is also a major point of interest for those considering migration from Marketing Cloud Engagement.

**Another important point is that AMPscript can be used not only in the code editor, but also directly within the visual editor**.

The experience of content creation and personalization is likely to change significantly more than ever before.

## Limitations in Preview Organizations

There are two types of AMPscript:

- **AMPscript Block:** `%%[ ... ]%%`
- **Inline AMPscript:** `%%= ... =%%`

However, **in the current preview organization, errors occurred when using AMPscript blocks**.

Therefore, at least at the current stage, the following types of processing may still not be available:

- **Variable assignment** (`VAR / SET`)
- **Conditional branching** (`IF THEN`)
- **Loop processing** (`FOR LOOP`)

On the other hand, based on the following image published by Salesforce, my understanding is that AMPscript blocks are ultimately expected to be supported as well.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*fwGXvtHLIAPT8V9E)

In addition, **some AMPscript functions are currently producing errors**.

For example:

- **SystemDateToLocalDate()**
- **RedirectTo()**

These areas may improve in future updates.

## About Marketing Object

Marketing Object is a feature that provides a “high-throughput, low-latency” data store for marketing data.

By importing CSV files, data from external systems can be brought into Marketing Objects.

Then, by using Handlebars or AMPscript to reference and manipulate Marketing Object data, email content personalization becomes possible.

The following permissions are required to manage Marketing Objects:

- **Manage Marketing Objects**
- **Modify All Records on Marketing Objects**
- **View All Records on Marketing Objects**

However, in the current preview organization, these permissions themselves are not yet displayed.

## Get Nobuyuki Watanabe @marketingcloudtips’s stories in your inbox

Join Medium for free to get updates from this writer.

Remember me for faster sign in

Therefore, my understanding is that the Marketing Object feature itself is still not available at this stage.

## Trying It Out

Since Marketing Objects are not currently available, I tested AMPscript without using any data source.

- Display the current time

%%=Now()=%%

_\*The system timezone is UTC._

- Display Japan time

%%=FormatDate(DateAdd(Now(),9,"H"), "yyyy年MM月dd日 HH時mm分")=%%

- Display a random number

%%=Random(1,100)=%%

- Convert a string to uppercase

%%=Uppercase("marketing cloud")=%%

- Concatenate strings

%%=Concat("Hello", " ", "World")=%%

- Get string length

%%=Length("Salesforce")=%%

- Change date format

%%=FormatDate(Now(), "yyyy/MM/dd")=%%

- Pseudo conditional branching

%%=Iif(Random(1,10) > 5, "YES", "NO")=%%

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/1*ItdHvaCwZ-jCSqIK5nBWoA.png)

When previewed, it is displayed as follows.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/1*Ez7zodUKnpaOvlokdCf89g.png)

What did you think?

Since this verification was conducted in a preview organization, the specifications and supported functionality may change by the time of the official release.

In particular, AMPscript blocks and the Marketing Object functionality are areas that seem likely to change significantly in future updates, so I would like to continue monitoring them.

That is all for this time.

Stay tuned for more Salesforce Marketing Cloud tips! 😎

## Nobuyuki Watanabe

[https://www.linkedin.com/in/nobuyuki-watanabe/](https://www.linkedin.com/in/nobuyuki-watanabe/) (+Follow me)

title: SFMC Tips #281 : Marketing Cloud Next: How to Set Up Conversational Email
markdown:
[

![Nobuyuki Watanabe @marketingcloudtips](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fill:40:40/1*zLRnWqPVgEgwGDwBBiwV3w.png)



](/@marketingcloudtips?source=post_page---byline--0ef29d7fd113---------------------------------------)

15 min read

Apr 24, 2026

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*hOST2c-qgVFmfWvh)

Photo by [Tim Mossholder](https://unsplash.com/@timmossholder?utm_source=medium&utm_medium=referral) on [Unsplash](https://unsplash.com/?utm_source=medium&utm_medium=referral)

**Conversational Email** has been introduced as a [Spring ’26 new feature](/@marketingcloudtips/marketing-cloud-next-spring-26-release-highlights-24c0c804b0cb) of **Marketing Cloud Next Advanced Edition** (\*this time, Growth Edition is not included).

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*Aa6JBSC9s8Sk6WCb)

This is the mechanism that Salesforce frequently announces as “**two-way email**”, and simply put, it is a function in which the Agentforce service agent automatically generates responses using generative AI to customer replies to emails.

Interactions with AI have already become common in chat and similar channels, but the fact that this can be realized via email can be considered a very new experience.

In my previous articles, I have introduced methods for embedding chat into [websites](/@marketingcloudtips/sfmc-tips-222-agentforce-deploying-service-agents-to-your-website-8b5f38c6b0b9) and [LINE](/p/e99698ef8d9c), but in this article, I will explain how to embed this mechanism into email.

## Main Use Cases of Conversational Email

Conversational Email can be utilized throughout the entire customer lifecycle and evolves traditional one-way notifications into interactive experiences.

The main use cases illustrated by Salesforce are as follows:

- **Promote event participation:** Easily respond with attendance status or preferences via reply
- **Cart abandonment measures:** Resolve pre-purchase questions via reply and prevent drop-off
- **Post-purchase follow-up:** Support initial usage and personalize experiences
- E**nhancement of transactional emails:** Request support by replying to delivery or billing notifications
- **Service reservations:** Easily select date and time via reply
- **Order management:** Handle return requests and check delivery status via reply
- **Loyalty improvement:** Provide benefits and offers through conversations
- **Product recommendations:** Personalized suggestions based on purchase history
- **Account linkage:** Improve efficiency for replenishment and inventory management via email replies

Indeed, it would be helpful if generative AI could handle all of these use cases.

I also recommend watching the following webinar.

## Overview of Conversational Email Implementation

For implementing Conversational Email, the following actions are required:

1. **Mechanism to escalate inquiries that Agentforce cannot handle**
2. **Basic configuration of the service agent**
3. **Enable conversation functionality for each sender address**
4. **Creation of conversational campaigns and flows**

In this article, I will explain this entire flow as carefully as possible. It will be a slightly long article, but I will not split it midway and will compile it as a single article. If you are interested, please read through to the end.

## 1. Escalation Mechanism

Now, the reason why we start with this configuration is that in the service agent settings, if there is no escalation flow, the agent cannot be activated. Even for those who think “escalation is unnecessary”, this is a required task.

Since this section is a bit long, I will summarize the flow:

1. **Enable Omni-Channel settings**
2. **Enable Messaging settings**
3. **Confirm Service Channels**
4. **Configure Routing**
5. **Configure Queue**
6. **Configure Presence Status**
7. **Configure and assign Presence Status Permission Sets**
8. **Configure Omni-Channel Flow**
9. **Configure Omni-Channel in Service Console**
10. **Confirm Service Console**

Now, let’s proceed.

## 1. Enable Omni-Channel Settings

Open “**Omni-Channel Settings**” from Setup and check “**Enable Omni-Channel**”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*NYF26wL8-LUa2EVZ)

## 2. Enable Messaging Settings

Open “Messaging Settings” from Setup and check Enable “**Messaging**”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*s_Grojtxnyfelx1k)

## 3. Confirm Service Channel

1\. Open \[**Setup**\] > \[**Service Channels**\] and check the list.

In SDO or free developer orgs, a service channel for “**Messaging Session**” with a specific name is created, so confirm that name.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*SN7CznvJdRTID4aQ)

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*nVUuGRFhZGbU7g2y)

- In a free developer org: **Messaging**
- In a partner demo org (SDO): **LiveMessage**

If there is no service channel for the “**Messaging Session**” object, create a new one.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*zawiYBCuQ9xplySP)

_※ Only one service channel can be created per object._

## 4. Configure Routing

1\. Select **Routing Configulations** from Setup and click New.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*Wuijj1ve96otogrt)

2\. In the next screen, define **Routing Name** (e.g., **Escalation Routing**) and **API Name**, then input or select the following and save:

- **Routing Priority: 1**
- **Routing Model: Most Available**
- **Work Item Size: Inherited**
- **Units of Capacity: 1**

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*cmod4LgNsNHe4Iev)

> Important: The above settings are just an example. In production, configure routing according to your Service Cloud admin and company requirements.

## 5. Configure Queue

1\. Select “**Queues**” from Setup and click New.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*oAkUB0XZwYXcaYIy)

2\. In the next screen, define **Label** (e.g., **Escalation Queue**) and **Queue Name**, then input or select the following and save:

- **Routing Configuration: set “Escalation Routing”**
- **Supported Objects: Messaging Session**

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*yTwA2WhOy_4ZIl8C)

3\. Scroll down and select users (mainly **customer service agents**) as queue members, then save.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*2E23a2sMUKPSzVdm)

## 6. Configure Presence Status

1. Open \[Setup\] > \[Presence Status\] and prepare the following three statuses:

- **Available for Escalations … Online**  
  ⇒ Set Service Channel to “Case”
- **Busy … Busy**
- **Unavailable … Busy**

_※ In my SDO environment, many statuses are already prepared._

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*QCnLo7F6kuF0H1Gd)

2\. Configure as follows:  
_\*Select_ **_Cases_** _and_ **_LiveMessage_**_._

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/1*ucFLm0MXCLHaoodQm5xHRA.png)

_※ Presence Status indicates whether an agent is available. Omni-Channel routes work based on this status._

## 7. Presence Status Permission Set and Assignment

1. Create a new permission set to access Presence Status.

- Name: **Service Presence Statuses Access (Custom)**

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*BPeSFatTSjzZADdh)

2\. In App Settings, select “**Service Presence Statuses Access**”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*GWEuZ9WNI_4_vumS)

3\. Enable the three created Presence Statuses.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*A2LVNAU3V1M5ZP8c)

4\. After creating the permission set, assign it to users.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*5uV32QlrLpmAwLta)

5\. Assign it to customer service agents responsible for escalation.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*74mu4FZFqYWW1U57)

6\. Also confirm that “**Service Cloud User**” is enabled for the user.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*iZgufWCBHmrMvH3D)

## 8. Add Access Permissions for Service Agents

1. Create another permission set.

- Name: **Messaging Permissions (Custom)**

Open the permission set and select App Permissions.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*FI9uVcTHlRpeaI9F)

2\. Click Edit and select:

- **Agent Initiated Outbound Messaging**
- **Configure Messaging**
- **Messaging Agent**

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*oLyKuG-kZVjFJdm9)

3\. Assign this permission set to escalation users.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*PV3tVZa4xJ2V8Edy)

4\. Also assign the “**Messaging User**” permission set license (not the permission set itself).

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*mT-CMI8NjbvjB7oK)

## 9. Configure Omni-Channel Flow

1. Create a “**New Flow**” from Setup.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*Y4nkQxw771xBlwJ1)

2\. Select “**Omni-Channel Flow**”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*De-O_IW7iR9SLY4c)

3\. Add element → “**Route Work**”.

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:746/0*P7eaoYQnz_eeMwVm.png)

4\. Define **Label** and **API name**, and create a “**new resource**” for Record ID.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*nQbJWLZ0ep2XDg8F)

5\. Select “**Variable**”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*OUWmSH39RQ7eWsPQ)

6\. Configure:

- **API Name: recordId**
- **Data Type: Text**
- **Available for Input: Checked**

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*ZR_iYQ8qQB9kLT3O)

7\. Select “**LiveMessage**” service channel.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*U2n1VBDMMoF0RrsG)

8\. Routing Type: **Queue (default)**

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*FYqC3HgUmJq-msZZ)

9\. Queue ID: select “**Escalation Queue**”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*8wJdwBk4Y4wKLyJ-)

10\. Save and activate the flow (**Escalation Flow**).

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:785/0*zKHP7ywNEzkaemBs)

## 10. Configure Omni-Channel in Service Console

1. Open “**App Manager**” from Setup.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/1*mwT4h6JMiJM3z8ByQ_NT1g.png)

2\. Find “**Service Console**” → Edit.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*NrS0qlJ5O3jGyEIo)

3\. Add Cases to “**Navigation Items**”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*JP2dL_zo4wDdP1u3)

4\. Add “**Utility Items (Desktop Only)**”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*wmqlNqBMRFwH-o6u)

5\. Select “**Omni-Channel**”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*VDppSwXc83vUdoxA)

6\. Save.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*r97riV4LxNhMnpj2)

## 11. Confirm Service Console

1\. Confirm “**Omni-Channel**” appears in the “**utility bar**”.  
This enables escalation agents to receive escalated emails.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*vBziRwPXOLpBbCOk)

### Considerations

- When a conversation is escalated, agents can view the entire history and reply within the same thread.
- Replies received within 30 days from the last sent email continue to be routed based on rules.
- Agents cannot initiate new outbound conversations. They can only respond to assigned/escalated ones.

### Extended Conversation Component

2\. Open “**Edit Page**” from “Service Console” settings.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*kxSStMh8gu1G4foz)

3\. Remove the standard “**Conversation**” component.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*6ye-PlJZHR9pMY8s)

4\. Add “**Enhanced Conversation**” component and save.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*D7PDHtJNyZpm_myn)

5\. Agents can now respond to escalated emails from this screen.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*SAk-kuN5Ya0fyagA)

How was it?

## Get Nobuyuki Watanabe @marketingcloudtips’s stories in your inbox

Join Medium for free to get updates from this writer.

Remember me for faster sign in

That concludes the section on building the escalation mechanism. It was quite long.

Next, we will proceed with the configuration of the service agent.

## 2. Basic Configuration of the Service Agent

1\. Enable “**Einstein**” from “**Einstein Settings**”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*6hlmSe5iSZZQLwak)

2\. Next, refresh the screen once by pressing “**F5**”, then enable Agentforce from Setup > “**Agentforce Agents**”. After that, move to the new Agentforce Builder that was officially released in February 2026.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*42AiajTQaC_pBHpV)

3\. Click “**New Agent**”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*NPGaMtexfhejf85y)

4\. Select “**Agentforce Service Agent**”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*kZw-feH08oXWmX92)

5\. This time, I named it “**Agentforce Conversational Email Agent**”. Also create a user for Agentforce.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*0JCMilB2JZz6RqBi)

6\. When the agent is created, the Agentforce Builder configuration screen will be displayed. This time, for topic selection, keep the default topics for the service agent. Do not change anything.

Next, click the plus mark for “**Connections**” from the menu and click “**Add Connections**”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*8JMZwi7vP_9-Ady_)

7\. Select “**Marketing Email**” and add it to the agent.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*w1h6QyAZnvRtIwR6)

8\. As you can see in the “**Behavior**” menu, at this time, the agent supports **only plain text responses**.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*0qqBvPg97eZl6DBC)

9\. Select the “**Routing**” menu. In the Escalation Flow section, select the **Omni-Channel Flow (Escalation Flow)** created in Section 1.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*AyY54e6i63EAEehF)

10\. Move to the “**System**” menu and **delete the default Welcome Message**. There is no delete button, so remove the existing text. (※ This is currently a required step.)

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*eWQTLFup-bov6FSh)

11\. Save and Commit Version.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*rBFBGaZZb2kapYrD)

12\. Finally, activate the agent.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*7naP3gse9-8xl0K9)

## 3. Enable Conversation Function for Sender Address

1. Select Setup > “**Authenticated Domains**” and add a domain.

_※ If there is already an authenticated domain in use in production, you can use that as is._

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*wI99uFEK70W4IrJM)

2\. Follow the appropriate steps to add the sending domain.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*GsS1REXjynIKGl2H)

_※ For details on setting up a sending domain, refer to the following article._

3\. When the domain status becomes “**Active**”, click “**View Details**”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*-vArzXdH2ShXZ9Dd)

4\. Move to the “**Reply Mail Management (RMM)**” tab and configure reply mail management as usual.

> _You may wonder whether RMM configuration is required for Agentforce to reply to emails, but this is used to process normal incoming messages that are not consent requests, not part of ongoing conversations, or not part of a conversational campaign flow._

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*d9AJ1WItayPnzNxe)

_※ For RMM configuration, refer to the following article._

5\. Next, move to the “**From Addresses**” tab displayed on the same screen and select the sender address to be used for this Conversational Email.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*oNnClY_bl5_2tjT6)

_Note: Conversational Email configuration must be set for each From address. The “Sender Display Name” and “Sender Address” selected here will be used in future conversations with the agent._

6\. Next, click the “**Configure**” button for “**Agent Conversational Messaging**” in the “**Capabilities**” section.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*_6lqF4sjd-nfUIcb)

7\. Select the “**Routing Type**”.

- **Agentforce Service Agent:** Routes the conversation directly to a specific configured service agent
- **Omni Flow:** Uses flows to route messages based on complex business logic
- **Omni Queue:** Routes the conversation directly to a specific queue

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:688/0*LrbqpFY7COsB1_Jz.png)

8\. This time, select “**Agentforce Service Agent**” for the simplest routing. Select the service agent created earlier.

_※_ **_Only agents that are “activated” in Agentforce Builder_** _will be displayed here. If the agent is not displayed, activate it and try again._

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*kwtiFvXSpOZK--WG)

9\. For the Fallback Queue, select the **Escalation Queue** created in the previous section. After setting, click “Save”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*R2LnDM3zlYxYCXGC)

10\. Activate the configuration.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*bDmPjkOEordFGLPz)

11\. Next is the “**Resolve Remaining Incoming Messages**” section. This time, keep it as “**Use Default Domain Settings**”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*_NNGPBwwRf_Enxhc)

- **Use Default Domain Settings**: Apply existing reply mail management settings
- **Use Custom Settings**: Apply custom settings that override existing reply mail management
- **Use Agent Conversation Settings**: Use the routing logic configured earlier in Agent Conversational Messaging

_※ For this selection, refer to the help documentation below to determine which option is appropriate._

12\. After configuration, return to the Routing menu in Agentforce Builder, and you will see that the service agent is registered as a Marketing Email channel.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*G4Os3V1YPmdccqNK)

With this, the Conversational Email configuration is finally complete. Lastly, we will configure the campaign and flow. These can be created manually, but since we have the opportunity, let’s use the Campaign Creation Agent.

## 4. Create Campaign and Flow

1\. Finally, create a campaign and flow using Agentforce. This feature is explained in the following article, so first configure the Agentforce Employee Agent so that it works.

2\. Open the agent from the marketing app “Home” screen.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*B_VsD38rtUBAEUny)

3\. Enter the following text and send it. You will be asked whether to create a **two-way email campaign**, so request creation as a two-way campaign.

> Build me a campaign targeting all individuals with an appointment coming up in 2 days. Assume the email includes a CTA prompting recipients to reply to the email if they would like to reschedule their appointment.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*KTT6wyQIlL6Q3ThR)

4\. A brief will be proposed, so save it as is once.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*5t79_9FLWrAYW15S)

5\. Then, the brief should have “**Conversational Campaign**” checked. If it is not checked, request “Please check Conversational Campaign”.  
Next, click a “**Generate Campaign Preview**”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*MDcXAOFSMXqa_8AN)

6\. Review the created “**Preview**”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*0-liFsywiDgDmvYW)

7\. You should see that the final step includes a “**Forward to Agent**” element.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*92KkvS7lwlQkxA6o)

8\. The “Wait” element is a standard time-based wait, but ignore it, “save the campaign preview”, and create the campaign.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*c9QI6i-SYjPJqsWS)

9\. This will automatically generate both the **campaign** and **flow**. In Flow Builder, it should include a “**Wait Until Event**” element and a “**Forward to Agent**” element.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*HuEjxYh4gM4miH2r)

_Tips: The “Wait Until Event” element is configured to wait until an email reply is received. This is also a new feature in Spring ’26, and it can be used even in flows that are not conversational email._

10\. After that, set the segment, configure the published email, activate the flow, and send it.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*F3wiPGegHR-D4kfn)

Now, all that remains is to try replying to the sent email.  
Let’s reply to the email that was actually sent.

## 5. Reply to the Sent Email

1\. The email has arrived, so reply to it.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*m7kKJfLx5b4xWeWV)

_Google has a thread display feature, so by using_ **_thread view_**_, you can feel like you are chatting. Of course, even in mailers without thread display, you can perform conversational email as a normal email exchange._

2\. State that you want to create a case. (Example: **Create a case.**)

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*i7eX1H-O29-LKzZn)

3\. After several tens of seconds, a reply email is received. The first time, since the campaign flow with the wait event created earlier is triggered, and compared to chat, there is a sense that email sending processing takes time.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*zVRulTyO78rcVvQh)

4\. If you move to the flow, you can confirm that the flow is running.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*wFguudVZxlHLSUpx)

5\. For identity verification, you are asked for the registered email address, but since it is unclear which one, notify “**Escalation**”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*tjLr4F-VkcrW5DG2)

6\. Then it is transferred to a human agent.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*PjmVIWIMYt1Z3Hl8)

7\. Now move to the “**Service Console**” and click “**Omni-Channel**” in the utility bar.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*B5ep_ybVdKc9WsKQ)

8\. From Presence Status, select the green “**Available for Escalations**”.

(※ Be careful as a loud sound will play.) 🔊🔊🔊

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:563/0*yqSa10b7UV2FQzol.png)

9\. Then, you should find a “**Support Email Conversation**” sent earlier, so select it.

- The **green icon** is the “**Messaging Session**” record.
- If you are testing in an SDO, please be aware that various other test data may be automatically generated as well.

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:568/0*ktAX3KTY4HcbunfR.png)

10\. This allows you to interact with the customer via email from this screen.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:875/0*AHI1BwLXYHcy8diG)

Success!!

## Conclusion

How was it?

Although it was a very long setup, for those familiar with Agentforce service agents, it is simply a reuse of existing mechanisms, so it may have felt like a simple setup.

Operationally, since service agents are required as handlers for escalation, please design while considering staffing in addition to the basic setup.

That’s all for this time.

Stay tuned for more Salesforce Marketing Cloud tips! 😎

## Nobuyuki Watanabe
